admin_panel.notes: Login uses your TP-Link ID (email and password), not a local admin/admin credential. The TP-Link ID is set up during initial Deco app configuration. The web UI at 192.168.68.1 and tplinkdeco.net is a monitoring dashboard only — it shows network status and allows firmware updates but cannot configure WAN settings. All WAN and internet configuration must be done through the Deco app.

ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: Switching from another nbn provider? Check the username and password fields on this page and make sure they're both empty before continuing — they're not needed for Belong.
ethernet.wan_port_notes: The Deco X60 has two unlabelled Gigabit Ethernet ports. WAN port detection is automatic — the Deco detects which port is connected to a modem or nbn box and uses that as the WAN port. Either port can be used. If needed, a fixed WAN port can be set in the app via More > Advanced > WAN Port.
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from your nbn connection box (NTD, HFC box, or FTTC DPU) to either Ethernet port on the back of the primary Deco X60 unit.
Power on the Deco X60 and wait for the LED to pulse blue — this means the Deco is ready for setup.
Download the Deco app on your phone: search for 'TP-Link Deco' in the App Store or Google Play, then install it.
Open the Deco app and log in with your TP-Link ID. If you don't have one, tap 'Don't have an account?' to create a free account.
Tap to start setup and select 'Deco X60' as your model when prompted.
Follow the app's instructions to plug your Deco into the modem — the app will guide you to connect Deco to the modem's LAN port and power both on.
Connect your phone to the Deco's default Wi-Fi network when prompted. The default network name (SSID) is printed on the label on the bottom of the Deco unit.
On the 'Connect your Deco to the internet' screen, tap 'Internet Connection Type' and select 'Dynamic IP'. This is the correct setting for Belong nbn (IPoE/DHCP — no username or password required).
Confirm that IPTV/VLAN Settings is set to Off, then tap Next.
Set your Wi-Fi network name and password when prompted, then tap Next to complete setup.
Wait for the Deco to connect to the internet — the LED will turn solid green when successfully connected.

physical.wan_port_position: Two Ethernet ports on the rear of the device
physical.reset_button: Recessed pinhole button on the bottom of the unit

factory_reset.method: Press and hold the recessed Reset button on the bottom of the Deco unit for 1 second, then release. The LED will pulse yellow to confirm the reset is in progress. Alternatively, reset via the Deco app: open the app, go to the network map, select the Deco unit, and tap Remove.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset removes all network settings, Wi-Fi name/password, and TP-Link ID association. After reset the unit returns to factory defaults and must be re-configured via the Deco app. Because login uses your TP-Link ID (not a local password), 'restoring default credentials' means unlinking the device from your account — you simply log in with your TP-Link ID again during setup.

firmware.check_path: Deco app: More > Update Deco. Alternatively via web UI at 192.168.68.1: Advanced > System > Firmware Upgrade (supports both automatic update and manual firmware file upload).

app.notes: The Deco app is required for all initial setup and WAN configuration. The web admin panel at 192.168.68.1 is monitoring-only and cannot be used to change internet connection type.

setup.setup_notes: The Deco X60 is a mesh system configured via the TP-Link Deco app — not through a traditional web admin panel. All WAN settings including internet connection type are configured in the app during initial setup (wizard step 7) or changed post-setup via More > Internet Connection. The web UI at 192.168.68.1 / tplinkdeco.net is a status dashboard only. Belong uses IPoE/DHCP: select 'Dynamic IP' in the app, no username or password required. The two Ethernet ports are unlabelled and interchangeable — the Deco auto-detects which port connects to the nbn box.

setup_confidence.notes: Setup process confirmed via official PDF user guide (AU download, REV3.6.0). App navigation path (More > Internet Connection) confirmed via TP-Link FAQ. 'Dynamic IP' IPoE label confirmed via manual screenshot (step 7 of setup wizard). WAN port auto-detection confirmed via TP-Link FAQ 4252. Reset button location and 1-second hold duration confirmed in official manual (Appearance page). Web UI limitations (monitoring-only, no WAN config) confirmed via TP-Link community KB. -10 deduction: app-only device for WAN configuration, which limits the ability to provide a traditional admin panel walkthrough.
